page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table offers a comprehensive overview of the relationship between BaP and PCB118 exposure and various metabolic markers across different species at Time 1. This investigation is particularly focused on the influence of specific SNOMED CT concepts, namely “Abnormal body temperature (finding)” and “Ineffective thermoregulation (finding),” which serve as critical indicators of metabolic dysregulation in response to environmental contaminants.

page.content:
The table delineates both included and excluded SNOMED CT concepts, highlighting the methodological rigor in selecting relevant clinical findings for analysis. 

Furthermore, the metrics of RWI (Relative Weight Index) and DF (Dysfunction Factor) are employed to quantify the degree of metabolic disturbance observed in subjects exposed to these compounds. The inclusion of adipokines such as Adiponectin and Leptin, along with fatty acid-binding proteins (FABP-4) and enzymes like HSL (hormone-sensitive lipase), underscores the multifaceted nature of metabolic responses. By focusing on these parameters, the study elucidates the potential pathways through which BaP and PCB118 may modulate metabolic homeostasis, thereby contributing to a more profound understanding of their health implications. The findings presented herein are essential for informing future research directions and public health policies.
